title: StatusEffect
description: Schema for a single status effect in the Remaster Engine. v1.0.0: created per DEC-002 (8 canonical statuses). Per §7.5 of the design document.

id.description: Status effect identifier. Per DEC-002, 8 canonical statuses. Matches the value used in tech.schema.json augmentation.status and effects.status.

category.description: Whether the status hurts or helps the affected character. Detrimental statuses can be cleansed; beneficial cannot.

max_stacks.description: Maximum stacks of this status that can be on a character simultaneously. e.g., poison=1 (single stack), but a hypothetical 'regen' could be 5 for stronger healing.

duration.description: Default duration in turns. 0 means 'until removed' (e.g., stop). Each tick decrements by 1; reaches 0 = removed. Some statuses (e.g., poison) may have an alternative tick model via tick_phase.

tick_phase.description: When the status effect processes. START_OF_TURN: before the affected character's turn (e.g., poison damage). END_OF_TURN: after the affected character's turn (e.g., burn). ON_DAMAGE_TAKEN: triggers when affected takes damage (e.g., retaliate-style statuses). ON_ACTION: triggers when the affected tries to act (e.g., sleep check, stop). PASSIVE: no tick, just provides a flag (e.g., confuse's accuracy modifier).

can_resist.description: Whether the affected character can resist this status with their element's resist_element affinity.

resist_element.description: Which element resists this status. 'any' means the resist roll uses the affected character's own element. 'neutral' means no element resists. Per §7.5.

action.description: The handler action. Format: <verb>:<target>. e.g., 'damage:hp:10', 'prevent:action', 'skip:turn', 'modify:accuracy:0.5'. Resolved by the StatusResolver at runtime.

handlers.description: List of handlers the status executes. e.g., poison might have ON_TICK/damage:hp:5 and ON_APPLY/announce. sleep has ON_TURN_START/prevent:action with chance roll.
